[{"idx": 0, "aspects": [{"aspect": "player_freedom", "sentiment": "positive", "evidence": "The biggest strength is how much player freedom and agency it gives you. You can solve problems through combat, stealth, persuasion, absurd physics, or just creative nonsense the devs somehow accounted for."}, {"aspect": "reactivity", "sentiment": "positive", "evidence": "The game constantly reacts to what you do in ways that feel genuinely handcrafted rather than scripted."}, {"aspect": "combat", "sentiment": "positive", "evidence": "Combat is turn-based but incredibly flexible. Positioning, abilities, environmental interactions, and party synergy all matter, and even fights you \"mess up\" can turn into memorable chaos instead of just a reload moment."}, {"aspect": "writing", "sentiment": "positive", "evidence": "And then there's the writing and characters. The companions are a huge highlight\u2014well-voiced, deeply written, and reactive to your decisions in ways that actually change relationships and outcomes."}, {"aspect": "characters", "sentiment": "positive", "evidence": "The companions are a huge highlight\u2014well-voiced, deeply written, and reactive to your decisions in ways that actually change relationships and outcomes."}, {"aspect": "choice_consequence", "sentiment": "positive", "evidence": "Choices actually matter in meaningful ways"}, {"aspect": "replayability", "sentiment": "positive", "evidence": "Huge amount of content and replayability"}, {"aspect": "complexity", "sentiment": "negative", "evidence": "The biggest barrier for some players is the sheer complexity and pacing. The game is dense.
